steps.title: Step 1: write the frequency table
steps.body:
We use the sibling-count survey of 20 students from the previous lesson:

| $x$ (siblings) | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| $f$ (frequency) | 4 | 8 | 5 | 2 | 1 |

Check the frequencies sum to the number of students: $4+8+5+2+1 = 20$. Good, $n = 20$.

steps.title: Step 3: compute the mean
steps.body:
$$\bar{x} = \frac{\sum fx}{\sum f} = \frac{28}{20} = 1.4$$

So the mean number of siblings is $1.4$.

steps.title: Step 5: compute the variance and standard deviation
steps.body:
Using the population variance formula:

$$\sigma^2 = \frac{\sum fx^2}{n} - \bar{x}^2 = \frac{62}{20} - 1.4^2 = 3.1 - 1.96 = 1.14$$

$$\sigma = \sqrt{1.14} \approx 1.068$$

So the standard deviation of the number of siblings is approximately $1.07$ (3 s.f.).
